title: ROI of AI: what actually works vs. what doesn't
post:
I've deployed AI across multiple businesses. Here's what actually delivers ROI:

Works:
- Customer service automation (40-60% cost reduction)
- Content first drafts (3x faster output)
- Data extraction and summarization (90% time savings)
- Coding assistants (25-40% developer productivity gains)

Doesn't work (yet):
- Complex decision-making requiring judgment
- Creative work that needs genuine originality
- Anything requiring reliable factual recall
- Tasks with low error tolerance

The pattern: AI excels at structured, repetitive tasks with measurable success criteria. It struggles with unstructured problems requiring contextual understanding.

The ROI question isn't 'can AI do this?' It's 'can AI do this reliably enough to justify the risk?'

What's your AI ROI story?
